skill.name: vue-options-api-best-practices
skill.description: Guides Vue 3 Options API component patterns, with TypeScript, this binding, lifecycle hooks, props, events, and computed values.
skill.summary: Improve Vue Options API components with safer TypeScript and this-binding patterns.
skill.author: github.com/vuejs-ai

security_audit.summary: Static analysis reported 258 issues, but targeted review found they are false positives from Markdown examples and documentation links. The skill is documentation-only and contains no executable scripts, credential access, network exfiltration, or prompt injection attempts. Safe to publish.

low_findings.title: Static Analysis Findings Dismissed as Documentation False Positives
low_findings.description: The external command alerts are Markdown fences and inline backticks around Vue and TypeScript examples. The network alerts are reference links and one local fetch example. The weak crypto, certificate, and reconnaissance alerts match words such as key, KeyboardEvent, keyof, and documentation metadata. No executable scripts, credential access, or data exfiltration behavior was found.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The flagged content is inside Markdown documentation or reference links, not runnable marketplace skill code. Targeted review found no prompt injection, shell execution, credential access, or external data transfer instructions.

content.value_statement: Vue Options API projects can lose type safety and correct this binding. This skill gives direct patterns for safer Vue components.
actual_capabilities: Explains when Options API methods and lifecycle hooks need regular functions.
Shows TypeScript patterns for defineComponent, props, events, and computed properties.
Documents provide and inject typing limitations in Options API components.
Describes stateful method risks such as shared debounced functions.
Points users to focused reference files for each Vue Options API issue.
limitations: Focuses on Vue Options API, not Composition API architecture.
Provides guidance and examples, not automated code transformation.
Assumes users already understand basic Vue component structure.
Some TypeScript guidance depends on the project Vue and TypeScript versions.

use_cases.title: Fix This Binding Errors
use_cases.description: Use the lifecycle and methods references to find arrow functions that break component instance access.

use_cases.title: Strengthen TypeScript Components
use_cases.description: Apply defineComponent, PropType, strict mode, and explicit return types in existing Options API components.
use_cases.target_user: Frontend engineers migrating Vue code to TypeScript

use_cases.title: Review Legacy Vue Code
use_cases.description: Check older Options API components for shared stateful methods, weak event typing, and outdated validator patterns.
use_cases.target_user: Maintainers of Vue 2 or Vue 3 applications

prompt_templates.title: Find This Binding Issue
prompt_templates.prompt: Review this Vue Options API component and explain why this is undefined. Focus on methods and lifecycle hooks. [Paste component]

prompt_templates.title: Add TypeScript Props
prompt_templates.prompt: Help me type these Vue Options API props with TypeScript. Include PropType guidance for objects, arrays, and functions. [Paste props]

prompt_templates.title: Improve Event Handlers
prompt_templates.prompt: Review these Options API event handlers. Recommend explicit event types and safer target handling. [Paste methods and template]

prompt_templates.title: Audit Component Practices
prompt_templates.prompt: Audit this Vue Options API component for this binding, strict TypeScript support, computed return types, and stateful methods. [Paste component]

output_examples.input: A mounted hook uses an arrow function and cannot read component data.
output_examples.output: The hook should use regular function syntax or method shorthand. Vue can then bind this to the component instance.

output_examples.input: A component accepts a user object prop and TypeScript infers a broad object type.
output_examples.output: Define a User interface and use Vue PropType for the prop. Add runtime validation if the object shape must be checked.

output_examples.input: A debounced method behaves incorrectly across repeated component instances.
output_examples.output: Create the debounced function per instance in a lifecycle hook. Cancel timers during unmount to avoid shared state and leaks.

best_practices: Use method shorthand or regular functions for Options API methods and lifecycle hooks.
Wrap TypeScript Options API components with defineComponent and enable strict checking.
Add explicit types for complex props, events, computed values, and injected properties.
anti_patterns: Using arrow functions as lifecycle hooks or top-level Options API methods.
Relying on untyped event parameters and broad object prop inference.
Creating shared debounced or throttled methods directly in the methods option.

faq.question: Why do arrow functions break Options API this binding?
faq.answer: Arrow functions keep this from their surrounding scope. Vue cannot replace it with the component instance.

faq.question: Does this skill cover Vue 3 Composition API?
faq.answer: No. It focuses on Options API components, including Vue 3 projects that still use Options API.

faq.question: When should I use defineComponent?
faq.answer: Use defineComponent for TypeScript Options API components. It improves inference for props, data, computed values, and methods.

faq.question: How should complex props be typed?
faq.answer: Define TypeScript interfaces and use Vue PropType for objects, arrays, functions, and union values.

faq.question: Can arrow functions be used inside methods?
faq.answer: Yes. Arrow callbacks inside a regular method can inherit the method this value safely.

faq.question: Does the skill run commands or modify files?
faq.answer: No. It is a documentation skill with Markdown references and does not execute commands.
